heading: GENERAL PROVISIONS
label: 1 GENERAL PROVISIONS
notes: Effect of 21st Amendment; Extent of Repeal of Title II of National Prohibition Act, , as amended and supplemented, known as the National Prohibition Act, was included in chapters 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title. Such provisions of that act and subsequent legislation as were dependent upon the 18th Amendment to the United States Constitution became inoperative by adoption of the 21st Amendment to the Constitution on , repealing the 18th Amendment.Act Oct. 28, 1919, ch. 8541 Stat. 305Dec. 5, 1933Sections 2 to 5 of this chapter (acts , ; , §§ 3, 5, ) and sections 11 to 40, 44 to 57, 61, 62, and 64 of chapter 2 (acts , §§ 2 to 39, to 319; , §§ 2, 5, , 223; , §§ 1 to 3, , 24) constituting title II of the National Prohibition Act as amended and supplemented, were repealed to the extent in force in the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands, Hawaii, and Alaska by acts , ; , §§ 2, 3, ; , §§ 2, 3, ; and , §§ 1, 5, , 584, respectively.Oct. 28, 1919, ch. 85, § 141 Stat. 307Nov. 23, 1921, ch. 13442 Stat. 223Oct. 28, 1919, ch. 8541 Stat. 308Nov. 23, 1921, ch. 13442 Stat. 222Mar. 31, 1933, ch. 1848 Stat. 23Jan. 24, 1934, ch. 4, § 148 Stat. 319Mar. 2, 1934, ch. 3748 Stat. 361Mar. 26, 1934, ch. 8848 Stat. 467Apr. 13, 1934, ch. 11948 Stat. 583
